name: Vic Damone
abstract: Vic Damone (born Vito Rocco Farinola; June 12, 1928 – February 11, 2018) was an American traditional pop and big band singer and actor. He was best known for his performances of songs such as the number one hit "You're Breaking My Heart", and other hits such as "On the Street Where You Live" (from My Fair Lady) and "I Have But One Heart".

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Porrello
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with him and actually I replaced Buddy Childers there. Buddy Childers was the trumpet player for Vic and I replaced him. I stayed for a couple of years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Porrello interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 6, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Duffy Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: I ran in and cut off the rehearsal... I went over the Vic’s drummer and I said, you may not know who I am but in around ten minutes I’m going to make my debut with the Count Basie orchestra, and I’m taking your cymbals and your sticks and your brushes, and incidentally what is your name please?
about_quotes.doc_title: Duffy Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 17, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Tommy Newsom and Ross Tompkins
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] And then we also did, for these same people, a Christmas album that was kind of interesting. There was five different singers — Vic Damone, Jack Jones, Joe Williams, Diane Carroll and Patti Page.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tommy Newsom and Ross Tompkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: I’m not a singer. You guys are singers – you and Vic Damone, Mel Tormé, Joe Williams, and Billy Eckstine. You guys are the singers.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: I did a bunch of stuff for Michelob beer for Vic Damone was the singer on some of those.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003
